status: completed (15 epochs so far, training continues)
motivation: V1 (Run 4) achieved val mIoU=0.813 with teacher forcing but only 0.547 on inference (ADE20k_Detecture) and 0.544 (RWTD at ep30). Qwen LoRA training degraded inference by -0.15 mIoU. Checkpoint comparison proved: epoch 5 (frozen Qwen) = 0.696, epoch 35 (trained Qwen) = 0.543. The Qwen LoRA overfits to ADE20K scene-level patterns.
hypothesis: Three-pronged attack: (1) Crop augmentation to simulate RWTD-style close-ups, (2) Cycle consistency to force projector to retain semantic info, (3) Constrained Qwen LoRA (r=4) to prevent overfitting.

front_1_crop_augmentation.description: Dynamic Macro-to-Micro crop in DetectureDataset.__getitem__. 35% probability random crop (300-600px), requires >=2 surviving textures, remaps indices/descriptions/embeddings contiguously.
front_1_crop_augmentation.effect: Prevents Qwen from expecting only full-scene images. Forces model to handle zoom-in texture crops like RWTD.

front_2_cycle_consistency.description: InverseProjector (256→4096) reconstructs tex_embeds from projected queries. cycle_loss = MSE(recon, gt.detach()). Gradient firewall: only updates InverseProjector + Projector, not Qwen/SAM.
front_2_cycle_consistency.effect: Forces 256-dim projection to retain semantic DNA. Active only in Phase 2 (cycle_weight=0.0 in Phase 1, 0.05 in Phase 2).

front_3_constrained_qwen.description: Reduced Qwen LoRA from r=16,alpha=32 to r=4,alpha=8. 4x less capacity to prevent domain overfitting.
front_3_constrained_qwen.effect: Qwen can fine-tune gently but cannot memorize ADE20K-specific patterns.

key_findings.finding: Crop augmentation extended Phase 1 learning beyond epoch 5 plateau
key_findings.evidence: V1 Phase 1 plateaued at epoch 5 (mIoU=0.696). V2 Phase 1 continued climbing to epoch 10 (mIoU=0.638). While lower absolute value (different config), the learning didn't stall — crop aug forces the model to handle diverse spatial scales.
key_findings.comparison: V1 Phase 1: 0.657→plateau at ep5. V2 Phase 1: 0.444→0.638 still climbing at ep10.

key_findings.finding: Test mIoU tracks val mIoU — no divergence
key_findings.evidence: RWTD test: 0.627(ep5) → 0.659(ep10) → 0.695(ep15). Val: 0.576(ep5) → 0.638(ep10) → 0.704(ep15). Both climbing together. In V1, test peaked at ep5 then DECLINED to 0.544 by ep30.
key_findings.comparison: V1: val/test diverged after Phase 2. V2: val/test track together. Crop augmentation prevents overfitting.

key_findings.finding: RWTD test mIoU reached 98% of ZS baseline
key_findings.evidence: Epoch 15 test mIoU = 0.695 vs ZS baseline 0.706. This is 98% — the closest any trained model has come to the zero-shot baseline. Previous best was 86% (V1 Run4 ep15).
key_findings.comparison: V1 best test: 0.678 (96% of ZS at ep5, dropped to 77% by ep25). V2: 0.695 (98% at ep15, still climbing).

key_findings.finding: Phase 2 transition smooth with constrained LoRA
key_findings.evidence: Val mIoU jumped 0.638→0.667→0.682→0.699→0.704 across epochs 10-15 (Phase 2). No destabilization. Cycle loss dropped 1.86→0.42 (InverseProjector learning). LM loss dropped 2.84→1.57.
key_findings.comparison: V1 Phase 2 also improved val but destroyed test. V2 Phase 2 improves both.

key_findings.evidence: Cycle loss started at 1.86 (epoch 11) and dropped to 0.42 by epoch 15. The InverseProjector quickly learned to reconstruct 4096-dim embeddings from 256-dim projections, confirming the projector retains semantic information.

key_findings.finding: Qwen generates excellent descriptions but wrong count
key_findings.evidence: Analysis of epoch 5 RWTD descriptions: when k_pred=2, descriptions are semantically accurate (mIoU=0.97). But 77% of samples get k_pred=6 (Qwen fills all slots). The 1-to-6 prompt encourages over-generation.

key_findings.finding: Prompt ablation: forcing exact count REDUCES mIoU
key_findings.evidence: '1 to 6' prompt: mIoU=0.660. 'exactly 2' prompt: mIoU=0.625 (-0.035). 'a few' prompt: mIoU=0.616. Hungarian matching rescues k=6 by selecting best 2 of 6 channels. Constraining count removes this selection benefit.

key_findings.finding: SAM channel collapse — descriptions are good but SAM ignores slot 2
key_findings.evidence: Deep analysis of 'exactly 2' prompt: Qwen descriptions are semantically perfect (correctly identifies both textures). But SAM assigns 98% of pixels to channel 1 and <2% to channel 2. The bottleneck is SAM's cross-attention, not Qwen's descriptions.

key_findings.finding: CRITICAL: SAM3 has extreme positional bias (Slot 1 Addiction)
key_findings.evidence: Positional bias test on 253 RWTD samples: Slot 1 gets 90.5% of pixels, Slot 2 gets 0.0% — regardless of content. Swapping T1↔T2 produces identical results. Even isolated runs (single query) always favor slot 1. SAM was pretrained for single-prompt and cannot resolve multiple competing queries.
key_findings.impact: This is the fundamental architectural bottleneck. All multi-texture segmentation collapses to single-channel because SAM's cross-attention ignores everything except position 1.

experiments_conducted.name: Checkpoint comparison (ADE20k_Detecture)
experiments_conducted.description: Tested epoch 5, 15, 35 checkpoints on 212 ADE20k_Detecture samples
experiments_conducted.result: Epoch 5 (P1): 0.696, Epoch 15 (P1): 0.691, Epoch 35 (P2): 0.543. Confirmed Qwen LoRA degradation.

experiments_conducted.name: Prompt ablation (RWTD)
experiments_conducted.description: Tested 3 prompt variants on epoch 10: '1 to 6', 'exactly 2', 'a few'
experiments_conducted.result: '1 to 6': mIoU=0.660, 'exactly 2': 0.625, 'a few': 0.616. More descriptions = better (Hungarian matching selects best K).

experiments_conducted.name: Positional bias test (RWTD)
experiments_conducted.description: Tested 3 conditions: baseline [T1,T2], swapped [T2,T1], isolated runs. Used GT embeddings directly.
experiments_conducted.result: All conditions: Slot1=90.5%, Slot2=0.0%. CONFIRMED extreme positional bias in SAM3 cross-attention.

experiments_conducted.name: Description quality analysis (epoch 5, RWTD)
experiments_conducted.description: Extracted Qwen generated descriptions and compared to GT for all 253 samples
experiments_conducted.result: When k_pred=2: descriptions are excellent (mIoU=0.97). Problem is count (77% predict k=6) and SAM channel collapse, NOT description quality.

next_steps_v3.primary: Batch Multiplexing — fix SAM3 positional bias by feeding each query independently in its own 'slot 1' position. Flatten (B, 7, 256) → (B*7, 1, 256) into batch dimension.
next_steps_v3.status: IMPLEMENTED in run_sam3_semantic, ready for next training run
next_steps_v3.expected_impact: Slot 2+ should now receive non-zero pixel allocation. Multi-texture segmentation should work for the first time.
next_steps_v3.verification: Re-run eval_positional_bias.py to confirm slot 2 allocation > 0%
